speaksAndFeeds.evaPanelistTitle: Eva Table (feedback to panelists and trainee)

alerts.cannotDeletePrincipal: The chair judge cannot be deleted.
alerts.principalExists: A chair judge already exists. Please change the current chair's role before assigning a new one.

timer.title: Timers
timer.doubleclickDing: Double click on ding to change number of sounds
timer.didYouHearDing: Did you hear the ding? (OK = Yes, Cancel = No)
timer.audioOptimized: Click 🎵 for optimized audio, or use the ding directly

athenas.prompt: Please enter a valid score between 50 and 100.
athenas.95-100:
Range: 95-100 points

Excellence: Probably one of the best debate speeches ever presented.

Rebuttal: It is incredibly difficult to think of satisfactory answers to any of the arguments presented.

Conviction: Perfect and totally convincing arguments.
athenas.92-94:
Range: 92-94 points

Quality: An incredible speech, undoubtedly one of the best the panel has ever seen.

Effectiveness: Engages incredibly effectively with the main issues of the debate, and presents exceptional arguments that require brilliant responses to be overcome.

Flaws: No significant errors or flaws in the speech.
athenas.89-91:
Range: 89-91 points

Brilliance: Brilliant arguments that engage very effectively with the main issues of the debate.

Explanation: The arguments are significantly well-explained and exemplified, and require extremely sophisticated responses to be defeated.

Issues: There are only minor issues, but they do not affect the strength of the arguments presented.
athenas.86-88:
Range: 86-88 points

Engagement: The arguments engage with the main issues of the debate and are highly convincing.

Logic: They have no logical flaws, and sophisticated responses are required to overcome the arguments.

Flaws: There are only small flaws or minimal errors, but they are almost insignificant in the arguments.
athenas.83-85:
Range: 83-85 points

Relevance: The arguments engage with the main issues of the debate and are very convincing.

Solidity: The arguments have very solid explanations that require strong responses to be overcome.

Response: They occasionally fail to respond sufficiently to well-constructed arguments, but the flaws in the speech are very limited.
athenas.79-82:
Range: 79-82 points

Pertinence: The arguments are relevant and engage with the main issues of the debate.

Construction: The arguments are well-constructed, lack obvious logical flaws, and are well-explained.

Vulnerability: The arguments could be vulnerable to good responses.
athenas.76-78:
Range: 76-78 points

Relevance: The arguments are mostly relevant and engage with most of the main issues of the debate.

Occasionality: Occasionally, but not frequently, the arguments may have flaws in some explanations; simplistic analysis that is vulnerable to competent responses.

Clarity: It is clear, so it can be followed and therefore credited.
athenas.73-75:
Range: 73-75 points

Pertinence: The arguments are mostly relevant, although they may fail to engage sufficiently with one or more relevant issues for the debate.

Simplicity: The arguments are logical, but tend to be simplistic, with some logical flaws and are vulnerable to competent responses.

Comprehension: It is clear enough to be followed and credited.
athenas.70-72:
Range: 70-72 points

Frequency: The arguments are frequently relevant.

Explanations: The arguments have some explanations, but there are regularly obvious and/or significant logical flaws.

Follow-through: It is sometimes difficult to follow and, therefore, to give credit to the entire speech.
athenas.67-69:
Range: 67-69 points

Relevance: Usually, the arguments are relevant.

Explanations: Almost all arguments have some explanations, but they have obvious and significant logical flaws.

Clarity: It is sometimes clear, but generally difficult to follow, and therefore, it is difficult to give much credit to the material presented.
athenas.64-66:
Range: 64-66 points

Presence: Some of the presented arguments are relevant.

Explanations: The arguments generally have few explanations, but they have significant logical flaws.

Comprehension: It is frequently unclear, which makes it difficult to give much credit to the speech.
athenas.61-63:
Range: 61-63 points

Occasionality: Occasionally some relevant claims are presented, and most are constructed and presented as arguments.

Presentation: Occasionally, the claims are presented as explanations, but they have very significant logical flaws.

Confusion: It is frequently unclear and confusing, which makes it difficult to give much credit to the speech.
athenas.58-60:
Range: 58-60 points

Relevance: Rarely are the claims relevant.

Construction: The claims are not constructed and presented as arguments; there may be some efforts to present explanations, but they are very vague.

Follow-through: It is very difficult to follow, which makes it difficult to give much credit to the speech.
athenas.55-57:
Range: 55-57 points

Presentation: A couple of almost never relevant claims are presented.

Reasoning: The claims are not constructed and presented as reasoning, they are more like observations.

Comprehension: The speech is difficult to follow almost in its entirety, which makes it difficult to give much credit.
athenas.50-54:
Range: 50-54 points

Content: The content is irrelevant.

Analysis: The content does not present anything that suggests analysis.

Follow-through: It is impossible to follow the speech in its majority, which makes it difficult to give any credit.

feedback.prompt: Please enter a valid score between 1 and 10.
feedback.10:
Exceptional

Accuracy: Extremely accurate results, reflected in a precise appreciation and meticulous evaluation of 'close' comparisons between teams; exhaustive recognition of all necessary team comparisons.

Reasoning: Extremely well-supported justification, evidenced through explanations presented with few to no flaws, which are deep, insightful, and nuanced; explicit identification and deep justification of metrics used in weightings, or criteria used for judging.

Discussion: Presents extremely astute and insightful comments on the debate; highly efficient, demonstrates an excellent ability to manage deliberation and (when appropriate), provides constructive feedback to the teams.
feedback.9:
Excellent

Accuracy: Very accurate results, reflected in a correct appreciation of 'close' comparisons between teams; exhaustive recognition of most necessary team comparisons.

Reasoning: Very well-supported justification, evidenced through clearly defined explanations that are deep, insightful, and nuanced; very good efforts to justify metrics used in weightings, or criteria used for judging.

Discussion: Presents very insightful comments on the debate; consistently efficient and demonstrates good judgment and effectiveness in managing deliberation.
feedback.8:
Very Good

Accuracy: Accurate results, reflected through a largely correct judgment on 'close' comparisons between teams; detailed recognition of most necessary team comparisons.

Reasoning: Justification presented in a comprehensible manner, evidenced through well-defined explanations that are deep and nuanced; very occasionally minor assumptions or personal biases in judging, or a minor lack of clarity in one or more team comparisons; the metrics used for judging are presented, but not explicitly justified.

Discussion: Mostly offers insightful comments on the debate, largely efficient, effective in managing deliberation.
feedback.7:
Good

Accuracy: Accurate results, reflected through generally correct positions, but potentially erroneous in relation to 'close' comparisons between teams; careful recognition of most necessary team comparisons.

Reasoning: Generally well-supported justification, evidenced through well-defined explanations; occasionally minor assumptions, personal biases in judging, or a minor lack of clarity in some team comparisons; the metrics used for judging are presented, but not justified.

Discussion: Generally presents relevant comments on the debate; efficient but with occasional errors and flaws, or prioritization issues in managing deliberation; demonstrates an appropriate level of judgment (sometimes limited) in providing feedback.
feedback.6:
Above Average

Accuracy: Mostly correct results, although there may be errors in relation to 'close' comparisons between teams.

Reasoning: Good attempt to justify the decision; explanations that demonstrate some degree of appreciation for the main clashes and how they are resolved; occasional assumptions or personal biases in judging that lead to minor flaws; lack of clarity in some team comparisons; not so clear on the metrics used.

Discussion: Presents some useful comments on the debate; to some extent inefficient and barely satisfactory management of deliberation; shows a lack of understanding of the main issues of the debate during feedback.
feedback.5:
Average

Accuracy: Generally accurate results, correct in most 'obvious' clashes; may fail in judging 'close' comparisons between teams, or may neglect a significant, but not fundamental, part of the debate.

Reasoning: Some effort to justify the decision; the explanations demonstrate some degree of appreciation for the fundamental clashes and issues; regularly with assumptions or personal biases in judging, some of which may affect the quality of the justification; lack of clarity regarding specific team comparisons.

Discussion: Sometimes inefficient in managing deliberation; occasionally has problems understanding one or more panel members without a clear reason for the difficulty; shows a significant lack of understanding of adjudication criteria and difficulty in providing feedback.
feedback.4:
Below Average

Accuracy: Inaccurate results, which, however, reflect an adequate identification of 'obvious' positions; the results reflect one or more incorrect interpretations or flawed responses; some lack of ability to identify important arguments or responses.

Reasoning: Unsatisfactory efforts to justify the decision; the explanations demonstrate some appreciation of the main clashes and issues of the debate, but do not justify the results presented, frequent assumptions or personal biases in judging, some of which affect the quality of the justification; lack of clarity regarding most team comparisons.

Discussion: Incompetence in managing deliberation; has problems considering or including all judges on the panel in the discussion; to some extent untimely in providing feedback.
feedback.3:
Poor

Accuracy: Inaccurate results that fail to correctly identify one or more 'obvious' positions; the results reflect multiple erroneous interpretations of the debate, some of which may be fundamental; some inability to follow important arguments or responses.

Reasoning: Poor efforts to justify the decision; the explanations demonstrate a lack of appreciation for the most important clashes and issues; frequent assumptions or personal biases in judging that can severely affect the results; lack of clarity regarding most team comparisons; the explanations are irrelevant to understanding the results.

Discussion: Great incompetence in managing deliberation; has problems considering or including all judges on the panel in the discussion; untimely and to some extent counterproductive in providing feedback.
feedback.2:
Very Poor

Accuracy: Significantly inaccurate results that completely fail to identify more than one of the 'obvious' positions; the results reflect several erroneous interpretations that are central to the debate; evident inability to follow important arguments and responses.

Reasoning: Little or no effort to justify the decision; the explanations demonstrate a lack of appreciation for the most important clashes and issues; frequent assumptions or personal biases or irrelevant perceptions that severely damage the quality of the justification and the results; lack of clarity regarding most team comparisons; the explanations are irrelevant to understanding the results.

Discussion: Great incompetence in managing deliberation; has problems considering or including the judges on the panel; untimely and to some extent counterproductive in providing feedback.
feedback.1:
Deplorable

Accuracy: Completely inaccurate result that totally fails to correctly identify at least one of the 'obvious' positions; the results reflect fundamental understanding problems in relation to the debate and the British parliamentary format; evident inability to follow important arguments and responses.

Reasoning: There is no reasonable attempt to justify the decision; the explanations demonstrate a lack of appreciation or a mistaken appreciation of the main clashes and issues of the debate; there are constantly assumptions or personal biases that significantly damage the quality of the justification and seriously affect the results; the explanations are irrelevant to understanding the results.

Discussion: Extreme incompetence in managing deliberation; has problems considering or including the judges on the panel; untimely and very counterproductive in providing feedback.

wsdc.prompt: Please enter a valid score between 60 and 80.
wsdc.80:
Exceptional (80 points)

Content: 32/40, Style: 32/40, Strategy: 16/20

An outstanding speech that sets the highest standard for the debate.
wsdc.76-79:
Excellent (76-79 points)

Content: 31/40, Style: 31/40, Strategy: 15-16/20

A very high quality speech with solid arguments and exemplary presentation.
wsdc.74-75:
Extremely Good (74-75 points)

Content: 30/40, Style: 30/40, Strategy: 15/20

A very strong speech with excellent content and delivery.
wsdc.71-73:
Very Good (71-73 points)

Content: 29/40, Style: 29/40, Strategy: 14-15/20

A solid speech with good arguments and effective presentation.
wsdc.70:
Good (70 points)

Content: 28/40, Style: 28/40, Strategy: 14/20

A competent speech that meets basic standards.
wsdc.67-69:
Satisfactory (67-69 points)

Content: 27/40, Style: 27/40, Strategy: 13-14/20

An adequate speech but with room for improvement.
wsdc.65-66:
Competent (65-66 points)

Content: 26/40, Style: 26/40, Strategy: 13/20

A basic speech that shows understanding of the format.
wsdc.61-64:
Pass (61-64 points)

Content: 25/40, Style: 25/40, Strategy: 12-13/20

A speech that needs development in several areas.
wsdc.60:
Improvement Needed (60 points)

Content: 24/40, Style: 24/40, Strategy: 12/20

A speech that requires significant work in all areas.

wsdcReply.prompt: Please enter a valid score between 30 and 40.
wsdcReply.40:
Exceptional (40 points)

Content: 16/20, Style: 16/20, Strategy: 8/10

An outstanding reply speech that brilliantly synthesizes the debate.
wsdcReply.37-39:
Very Good to Excellent (37-39 points)

Content: 15/20, Style: 15/20, Strategy: 7.5/10

A high-quality reply speech with effective synthesis.
wsdcReply.35-36:
Good (35-36 points)

Content: 14/20, Style: 14/20, Strategy: 7/10

A competent reply speech that meets basic standards.
wsdcReply.32-34:
Pass to Satisfactory (32-34 points)

Content: 13/20, Style: 13/20, Strategy: 6.5/10

An adequate reply speech but with room for improvement.
wsdcReply.30-31:
Improvement Needed (30-31 points)

Content: 12/20, Style: 12/20, Strategy: 6/10

A reply speech that requires significant work.

demian.prompt: Please enter a valid score between 50 and 100.
demian.excelente:
Excellent (95-100 points)

Construction: The work of the elements perfectly supports the construction of emotionality, script and relevance, which allows an ideal reception of the message by the empathetic receptor and, possibly, gives way to one of the best speeches in history.

Flaws: There are no flaws or those that exist are insignificant compared to the rest of the material delivered.
demian.sobresaliente:
Outstanding (89-94 points)

Construction: The work of the elements supports almost perfectly the construction of emotionality, script and relevance, which allows a wonderful reception of the message by the empathetic receptor and, possibly, gives way to one of the best speeches in the category.

Flaws: There are few flaws compared to the positive elements of the material delivered.
demian.muyBueno:
Very Good (86-88 points)

Construction: The work of the elements incredibly supports the construction of emotionality, script and relevance, which allows an excellent reception of the message by the empathetic receptor.

Flaws: There are minor flaws compared to the positive aspects of the speech.
demian.bueno:
Good (83-85 points)

Construction: The work of the elements supports very well the construction of emotionality, script and relevance, which allows a great reception of the message by the empathetic receptor.

Flaws: There are minor flaws compared to the positive aspects of the speech.
demian.satisfactorio:
Satisfactory (80-82 points)

Construction: The work of the elements correctly supports the construction of emotionality, script and relevance which allows a good reception of the message by the empathetic receptor.

Flaws: There are small flaws compared to the positive aspects of the speech.
demian.aceptable:
Acceptable (77-79 points)

Construction: There are minor flaws in some elements that partially affect the construction of emotionality, script and/or relevance which allows an appropriate reception of the message by the empathetic receptor.

Impact: There are flaws that slightly harm the content of the speech.
demian.regular:
Fair (74-76 points)

Construction: There are partial flaws in several elements that affect the construction of emotionality, script and/or relevance and, therefore, mitigate an optimal reception of the message by the empathetic receptor.

Impact: There are flaws that harm but not in a relevant way the content of the speech.
demian.deficiente:
Deficient (71-73 points)

Construction: There are partial flaws in most of the elements that affect the construction of emotionality, script and/or relevance and, therefore, do not allow a correct reception of the message by the empathetic receptor.

Impact: There are flaws that partially harm the content of the speech.
demian.malo:
Poor (68-70 points)

Construction: There are flaws in many of the elements that affect the construction of emotionality, script and relevance and, therefore, interrupt the reception of the message by the empathetic receptor.

Impact: There are flaws that greatly harm the content of the speech.
demian.muyMalo:
Very Poor (65-67 points)

Construction: There are partial flaws in all elements, or complete failures in a couple of elements that affect the construction of emotionality, script and relevance and, therefore, strongly interrupt the reception of the message by the empathetic receptor.

Impact: There are flaws that seriously and significantly harm most of the content of the speech.
demian.insuficiente:
Insufficient (62-64 points)

Construction: There is work on some elements that seeks to build, without achieving it effectively, the pillars of emotionality, script and relevance, therefore, the message cannot be correctly received by the empathetic receptor.

Impact: There are flaws that seriously and significantly harm the content of the speech.
demian.inadecuado:
Inadequate (59-61 points)

Construction: There is work on some elements that seeks to build, without achieving it, some of the pillars of emotionality, script and relevance, therefore, part of the message cannot be received by the empathetic receptor.

Impact: There are complete failures that lower the level of the discursive material.
demian.pobre:
Weak (56-58 points)

Construction: There is work on a minority of elements that seeks to build, without achieving it, one of the pillars of emotionality, script and relevance, therefore, a large part of the message cannot be received by the empathetic receptor.

Impact: There are relevant flaws that negatively alter the discursive material.
demian.inaceptable:
Unacceptable (50-55 points)

Construction: There is no work on elements and no emotionality, script or relevance is built, therefore, there is no message that can be received by the empathetic receptor.

Impact: There are flaws that negatively affect the discursive material.

eva.prompt: Please enter a valid score between 1 and 10.
eva.excelente:
Excellent (10 points)

Appreciation: Precise attention and meticulous evaluation of speeches, exhaustive recognition of all manual criteria within the presentations, exceptional weighting of errors in relation to persuasion and quite strict identification of compliance with pillars and empathy construction.

Justification: Extremely well-supported reasoning, evidenced through deep and insightful explanations of appreciated elements. Justifies all their appreciations logically and evidencing a very deep knowledge of the manual.

Deliberation: Demonstrates excellent ability to manage deliberation and, when appropriate, adds comments that contribute significantly to the discussion, showing consistent openness to listening regardless of their evaluation of others. Is extremely efficient at mediating intervention times and ability to deal with tensions between panel members.

Room Management: Shows outstanding room management, adapting to dynamic instructions, preparation times and organizing speakers in the most efficient way. Stands out for their ability to resolve potentially problematic issues.
eva.sobresaliente:
Outstanding (9 points)

Appreciation: Quite detailed attention and correct evaluation of speeches, exhaustive recognition of the vast majority of manual criteria within presentations, excellent weighting of errors in relation to persuasion and strict identification of compliance with pillars and empathy construction.

Justification: Very well-supported reasoning, evidenced through detailed explanations of appreciated elements. Justifies the vast majority of their appreciations logically and evidencing deep knowledge of the manual.

Deliberation: Demonstrates great ability to manage deliberation and, when appropriate, adds comments that contribute to the discussion, showing general openness to listening. Very occasionally imposes their vision within the panel. Is very efficient at mediating intervention times and ability to deal with tensions between panel members.

Room Management: Shows very good room management, adapting to dynamic instructions, preparation times and efficiently organizing speakers. Has great skills to resolve potentially problematic issues.
eva.muyBueno:
Very Good (8 points)

Appreciation: Detailed attention and correct evaluation of most speeches, good recognition of most manual criteria within presentations, very good weighting of errors in relation to persuasion and good identification of compliance with dynamic relevance.

Justification: Well-supported reasoning. Justifies most of their appreciations logically and evidencing good knowledge of the manual. Some of their explanations may be unclear or slightly deviate from an empathetic receptor's perspective.

Deliberation: Demonstrates sufficient ability to manage deliberation and, when appropriate, adds comments that generally contribute to the discussion, showing openness to listening at multiple moments. Occasionally may act seeking consensus and tries to impose their vision within the panel. Is efficient at mediating intervention times and ability to deal with tensions between panel members, although sometimes may give more space than necessary to some people or speeches.

Room Management: Shows good room management, generally adapting to dynamic instructions and preparation times. Also efficiently organizes speakers, but with small management flaws. Has good skills to resolve potentially problematic issues.
eva.bueno:
Good (7 points)

Appreciation: Shows correct attention and evaluation of most speeches, although at certain moments may omit important points in applying manual criteria, good weighting of errors in relation to persuasion and correct identification of compliance with pillars and empathy construction.

Justification: Mostly well-supported reasoning. Justifies several of their appreciations logically and evidencing clear knowledge of most of the manual. Their explanations may be unclear or not explicit, additionally, may deviate from an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying some criteria.

Deliberation: Demonstrates ability with some minor flaws to manage deliberation and adds comments that generally contribute partially to the discussion, showing openness to listening at multiple moments. Sometimes may add comments at inappropriate moments, interrupting people or reopening closed discussions without justifications. May act seeking forced consensus or tries to impose their vision within the panel in some aspects. Is partially efficient at mediating intervention times and dealing with tensions between panel members, although may tend to give more space than necessary to some people or speeches, or give themselves more time than the rest of the panel.

Room Management: Shows decent room management, partially adapting to dynamic instructions and preparation times. Has small flaws when organizing speakers. Has minor setbacks in resolving potentially problematic issues.
eva.satisfactorio:
Satisfactory (6 points)

Appreciation: Shows sufficient attention and evaluation of most speeches, although at certain moments may misunderstand elements of manual criteria or not recognize the most relevant aspects of presentations. Their error weighting may fail in relation to persuasion and correctly identifies compliance with pillars and empathy construction, but occasionally gives less priority to some criteria compared to others.

Justification: Some well-supported reasoning. Justifies some of their appreciations logically and evidencing partial knowledge of parts of the manual. Their explanations tend to be unclear or merely implicit. Additionally, tends to deviate from an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying some criteria.

Deliberation: Demonstrates ability, with some flaws, to manage deliberation and adds comments that may contribute partially to the discussion, showing openness to listening at some moments. Generally gives comments that, while correct, may be at inappropriate moments, interrupting people or reopening closed discussions without justifications. May act seeking forced consensus and tries to impose their vision within the panel in various aspects. Is partially efficient at mediating intervention times or dealing with tensions between panel members, but may fail seriously in one of the two aspects.

Room Management: Shows partially good room management and adapts partially to dynamic instructions and preparation times, but with moderately relevant problems in one of the two points. Has problems when organizing speakers. Has setbacks in resolving potentially problematic issues.
eva.regular:
Fair (5 points)

Appreciation: Shows sufficient attention and evaluation of most speeches, although may misunderstand elements of manual criteria or not recognize the most relevant aspects of presentations in a relevant number of speeches. Their error weighting may have problems, failing to recognize the impact of negative elements. Correctly identifies compliance with pillars and empathy construction, but occasionally gives less priority to some criteria compared to others.

Justification: Some reasoning is supported. Justifies some of their appreciations but with explanations that may be incoherent and/or evidence lack of knowledge of parts of the manual. Their explanations may be confusing, additionally, deviates from an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying most criteria, employing perspectives that are heavily biased by the judge's particular subjectivity.

Deliberation: Demonstrates ability with multiple flaws to manage deliberation. Adds comments that may be disruptive to the conversation, being these at inappropriate moments or disrespectful to some speech or judge. Has problems closing discussions, leaving them incomplete when moving to other topics. Tends to seek forced consensus and may impose their vision within the panel regarding each speaker's scores. Is inefficient at mediating intervention times or dealing with tensions between panel members.

Room Management: Shows partial problems in room management, partially adapting to dynamic instructions and preparation times, but with moderately relevant problems in both points or severe problems in one of the points. Has relevant problems when organizing speakers. Has serious setbacks in resolving potentially problematic issues.
eva.deficiente:
Deficient (4 points)

Appreciation: Shows lack of attention in several speeches, so their evaluation of evaluation criteria is incomplete in relevant points, not considering deep elements. Their error weighting shows they don't measure correctly how these affect a speech's persuasion. Fails to recognize compliance with pillars and empathy construction as factors to consider when evaluating a speech.

Justification: There are unsuccessful efforts to construct reasoning. Justifies some of their appreciations but with explanations that tend to be incoherent with each other or with what's stated in the manual, demonstrating general lack of knowledge or poor understanding of it. Their explanations tend to be confusing, additionally, ignores an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying criteria, employing subjective perspectives heavily biased by the judge's particular subjectivity.

Deliberation: Demonstrates deficient ability to manage deliberation. Makes minor contributions in discussions, being a passive agent in deliberation. Seeks forced consensus and tends to their vision within the panel regarding each speaker's scores, or doesn't seek consensus of ideas and only passively listens to panel comments. Has little control over deliberation times, tending to cut discussions at inappropriate moments or exceed times for discussions to continue.

Room Management: Shows problems in room management, with adaptation problems to dynamic instructions and preparation times. Shows inability to manage speakers. Shows serious problems in resolving problematic issues.
eva.malo:
Poor (3 points)

Appreciation: Shows lack of attention in the generality of speeches, so their evaluation of evaluation criteria is incorrect and demonstrates lack of knowledge of speech content. Recognizes errors within speeches, but doesn't weigh them with positive aspects, instead evaluates them in abstract. Their evaluation of pillars and empathy construction is deficient, or ignores both factors when talking about speeches.

Justification: There are attempts to construct reasoning. Justifications are only for some of their appreciations and show lack of knowledge of the manual. There are few explanations, which are incoherent, extremely confusing and, additionally, completely ignore an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying criteria, being carried away by ideas contrary to what's expressed in the manual.

Deliberation: Demonstrates inability to manage deliberation. Makes minor contributions in discussions, but these are disruptive to it, interrupting or tending to disrespect. Forces consensus within the panel regarding each speaker's scores, ignoring panel comments about their speeches. Has little control over deliberation times, tending to cut discussions at inappropriate moments or exceed times for discussions to continue. Additionally, their conflict mediation abilities are problematic, seeking not their resolution, but the imposition of their vision.

Room Management: Shows severe problems in room management, with serious adaptation problems to dynamic instructions and preparation times that the rest of the panel must take control of the room to give instructions. Shows inability to manage speakers. Shows no intentions to resolve problems, delegating this to the rest of the panel.
eva.pobre:
Weak (2 points)

Appreciation: Shows total lack of attention to all speeches, so their evaluation is built based on appreciations not linked to speeches or not associated with any speech evaluation criteria. Completely ignores speech errors, similarly, doesn't evaluate if these are persuasive. Doesn't know if speeches comply with pillars and empathy construction, leaving these criteria discarded in their evaluations.

Justification: There are no reasoning or attempts at them. There are no justifications for appreciations and if these exist, they are only based on subjective appreciations without foundations in the manual and with null depth.

Deliberation: Demonstrates extreme inability to manage deliberation. Doesn't contribute to discussion, letting deliberation be controlled by the rest of the panel.

Room Management: Doesn't manage the room, taking a totally passive role within it and acting as panel.
eva.inaceptable:
Unacceptable (1 point)

Appreciation: Shows total lack of attention to all speeches, so their evaluation is built based on appreciations not linked to speeches or not associated with any evaluation criteria beyond implicit elements. Fails to recognize errors within speeches, so their consideration in evaluation is low. Their evaluation of pillars and empathy construction is biased.

Justification: There are no reasoning or attempts at them. There are no justifications for appreciations and if these exist, they are only based on subjective appreciations without foundations in the manual and with null depth.

Deliberation: Demonstrates extreme inability to manage deliberation. Doesn't contribute to discussion, letting deliberation be controlled by the rest of the panel.

Room Management: Doesn't manage the room, taking a totally passive role within it and acting as panel.

evaPanelist.prompt: Please enter a valid score between 1 and 10.
evaPanelist.excelente:
Excellent (10 points)

Appreciation: Precise attention and meticulous evaluation of speeches, exhaustive recognition of all manual criteria within presentations, exceptional weighting of errors in relation to persuasion and quite strict identification of compliance with dynamic relevance.

Justification: Extremely well-supported reasoning, evidenced through deep and insightful explanations of appreciated elements. Justifies all their appreciations logically and evidencing a very deep knowledge of the manual.

Deliberation: Outstanding contribution during deliberation and, when appropriate, adds comments that contribute significantly to the discussion. Their interventions allow for fluid discussion without showing any sign of imposing their evaluation on others.
evaPanelist.sobresaliente:
Outstanding (9 points)

Appreciation: Quite detailed attention and correct evaluation of speeches, exhaustive recognition of the vast majority of manual criteria within presentations, excellent weighting of errors in relation to persuasion and strict identification of compliance with dynamic relevance.

Justification: Very well-supported reasoning, evidenced through detailed explanations of appreciated elements. Justifies the vast majority of their appreciations logically and evidencing deep knowledge of the manual.

Deliberation: Very valuable contributions during deliberation and, when appropriate, adds comments that contribute to the discussion positively. Their interventions are accurate on relevance issues without showing signs of imposing their evaluation on others.
evaPanelist.muyBueno:
Very Good (8 points)

Appreciation: Detailed attention and correct evaluation of most speeches, good recognition of most manual criteria within presentations, very good weighting of errors in relation to persuasion and good identification of compliance with dynamic relevance.

Justification: Well-supported reasoning. Justifies most of their appreciations logically and evidencing good knowledge of the manual. Some of their explanations may be unclear or slightly deviate from an empathetic receptor's perspective.

Deliberation: Valuable contributions to deliberation, offering comments that, for the most part, are relevant to the discussion. Manages quite positive criteria, although in certain interventions their understanding of consensus-free evaluation may be misinterpreted.
evaPanelist.bueno:
Good (7 points)

Appreciation: Shows correct attention and evaluation of most speeches, although at certain moments may omit important points in applying manual criteria, good weighting of errors in relation to persuasion and correct identification of compliance with dynamic relevance.

Justification: Mostly well-supported reasoning. Justifies several of their appreciations logically and evidencing clear knowledge of most of the manual. Their explanations may be unclear or not explicit, additionally, may deviate from an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying some criteria.

Deliberation: Good contributions to deliberation. Offers comments that, despite being relevant to the discussion, may be disruptive to the discussion. May occasionally present minor attention failures.
evaPanelist.satisfactorio:
Satisfactory (6 points)

Appreciation: Shows sufficient attention and evaluation of most speeches, although at certain moments may misunderstand elements of manual criteria or not recognize the most relevant aspects of presentations. Their error weighting may fail in relation to persuasion and correctly identifies compliance with dynamic relevance, but occasionally gives less priority compared to other criteria.

Justification: Some well-supported reasoning. Justifies some of their appreciations logically and evidencing partial knowledge of parts of the manual. Their explanations tend to be unclear or merely implicit. Additionally, tends to deviate from an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying some criteria.

Deliberation: Positive contributions to most of the deliberation. Usually makes comments that, for the most part, may be less relevant to the discussion, although covers central topics to discuss. Their participations may be occasionally inappropriate or wrong, especially due to unnecessary replies that tend to point toward consensus.
evaPanelist.regular:
Fair (5 points)

Appreciation: Shows sufficient attention and evaluation of most speeches, although may misunderstand elements of manual criteria or not recognize the most relevant aspects of presentations in a relevant number of speeches. Their error weighting may have problems, failing to recognize the impact of negative elements. Correctly identifies compliance with dynamic relevance, but may fail to recognize relevance in relation to the assigned topic.

Justification: Some reasoning is supported. Justifies some of their appreciations but with explanations that may be incoherent and/or evidence lack of knowledge of parts of the manual. Their explanations may be confusing, additionally, deviates from an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying most criteria, employing perspectives heavily biased by the judge's particular subjectivity.

Deliberation: Average contribution during deliberation. Their participations may cover central topics of discussion. May occasionally make comments that interrupt or attempt to minimize other people's interventions without justification.
evaPanelist.deficiente:
Deficient (4 points)

Appreciation: Shows lack of attention in several speeches, so their evaluation of evaluation criteria is incomplete in relevant points, not considering deep elements. Their error weighting shows they don't measure correctly how these affect a speech's persuasion. Fails to recognize compliance with dynamic relevance as a factor to consider when evaluating a speech.

Justification: There are unsuccessful efforts to construct reasoning. Justifies some of their appreciations but with explanations that tend to be incoherent with each other or with what's stated in the manual, demonstrating general lack of knowledge or poor understanding of it. Their explanations tend to be confusing, additionally, ignores an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying criteria, employing subjective perspectives heavily stained by the judge's particular subjectivity.

Deliberation: Average contribution during deliberation that may occasionally be irrelevant. On several occasions makes comments that interrupt other people's interventions without justification.
evaPanelist.malo:
Poor (3 points)

Appreciation: Shows lack of attention in the generality of speeches, so their evaluation of evaluation criteria is incorrect and demonstrates lack of knowledge of speech content. Recognizes errors within speeches, but doesn't weigh them with positive aspects, instead evaluates them in abstract. Their evaluation of dynamic relevance is deficient in relation to the presented dynamic, or is ignored when talking about speeches.

Justification: There are attempts to construct reasoning. Justifications are only for some of their appreciations and show lack of knowledge of the manual. There are few explanations, which are incoherent, extremely confusing and, additionally, completely ignore an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying criteria, being carried away by ideas contrary to what's expressed in the manual.

Deliberation: Below-average contribution during deliberation. Their few participations contribute little to the discussion. Their comments are disruptive and point to disrespect.
evaPanelist.pobre:
Weak (2 points)

Appreciation: Shows serious lack of attention in the generality of speeches, so their evaluation is based on isolated appreciations of them and is not associated with evaluation criteria beyond implicit elements. Fails to recognize errors within speeches, so their consideration in evaluation is low. Their evaluation of relevance is biased and ignores the round's dynamic.

Justification: There are minor attempts to construct some reasoning. Justifications are minimal for appreciations and show lack of knowledge of the manual, as they tend to contradict it. There are very few explanations, which are extremely incoherent and confusing and, additionally, completely ignore an empathetic receptor's perspective when justifying criteria, being carried away by ideas contrary to what's expressed in the manual.

Deliberation: Deficient contribution during deliberation. Little or no intention to participate in discussion. Their few comments may lack relevance to the discussion's substance, are disruptive and disrespectful.
evaPanelist.inaceptable:
Unacceptable (1 point)

Appreciation: Shows total lack of attention to all speeches, so their evaluation is built based on appreciations not linked to speeches or not associated with any speech evaluation criteria. Completely ignores speech errors, similarly, doesn't evaluate if these are persuasive. Doesn't know if speeches are relevant to the dynamic, leaving this criterion discarded from their evaluations.

Justification: There are no reasoning or attempts at them. There are no justifications for appreciations. There are no explanations, and if these exist, they are only based on subjective appreciations without foundations in the manual and with null depth.

Deliberation: Doesn't participate, doesn't contribute comments or, when asked to intervene, they are constructions completely irrelevant to the discussion.

panel.prompt: Please enter a valid score between 1 and 10.
panel.10:
Exceptional

Role: Extraordinarily well-adapted to the expected role; evident awareness and response to the needs of the head judge and the room.

Contribution: Extremely useful and insightful contributions in the deliberation; offers perspectives and arguments that significantly enrich and improve the quality of the debate and the final decision.

Communication: Exceptionally clear, concise, and respectful communication; demonstrates active listening and an outstanding ability to build on the ideas of others, fostering an environment of collaboration and intellectual rigor.
panel.9:
Excellent

Role: Very well-adapted to the expected role; demonstrates a strong awareness of the needs of the head judge and the room.

Contribution: Very useful and insightful contributions; consistently offers perspectives that improve the quality of the deliberation.

Communication: Very clear and effective communication; actively participates and contributes to a constructive dialogue.
panel.8:
Very Good

Role: Well-adapted to the expected role; generally aware of the needs of the head judge and the room.

Contribution: Useful contributions that frequently aid the deliberation; occasionally presents arguments that refine the understanding of the debate.

Communication: Clear and mostly effective communication; good level of participation and collaboration.
panel.7:
Good

Role: Adequately adapted to the role, with some minor inconsistencies; demonstrates a basic awareness of the head judge's needs.

Contribution: Generally relevant contributions, although they do not always significantly impact the deliberation; may offer good ideas, but they need further development.

Communication: Generally clear communication, but there may be moments of ambiguity; participates appropriately, but could be more proactive.
panel.6:
Above Average

Role: Partial adaptation to the role; sometimes does not respond to the needs of the head judge or the deliberation.

Contribution: Some contributions are useful, but others may be irrelevant or repetitive; has the potential to contribute more effectively.

Communication: Communication is functional, but sometimes lacks clarity or conciseness; participation is inconsistent.
panel.5:
Average

Role: Minimal adaptation to the expected role; often out of sync with the head judge's needs.

Contribution: Contributions are sporadic and rarely constructively influence the deliberation; the ideas presented are often superficial.

Communication: Communication is often unclear or difficult to follow; participation is passive or disruptive.
panel.4:
Below Average

Role: Poor adaptation to the role; demonstrates little or no understanding of what is required in the deliberation.

Contribution: Contributions are mostly irrelevant or counterproductive; does not help to advance the deliberation.

Communication: Communication is consistently unclear; participation is minimal or non-existent.
panel.3:
Poor

Role: Very poor adaptation to the role; actions go against the needs of a productive deliberation.

Contribution: Contributions, if any, are detrimental to the deliberation; introduces confusion or incorrect information.

Communication: Communication is confusing and obstructs dialogue; participation is negative.
panel.2:
Very Poor

Role: No adaptation to the role; actively undermines the deliberation process.

Contribution: No constructive contributions; their presence is an obstacle to reaching a decision.

Communication: Communication is non-existent or totally ineffective; does not participate or participation is destructive.
panel.1:
Deplorable

Role: Shows contempt for the role and responsibilities of a panelist; their behavior is completely inappropriate.

Contribution: Any intervention is actively harmful, showing a total disinterest in the adjudication process.

Communication: Refuses to communicate or does so in a way that prevents any form of deliberation.

breakPredict.title: Break Predict
breakPredict.addTeamsHint: Add teams using the form above
breakPredict.instruction1: Add teams in the Teams tab first
breakPredict.instruction2: Click 'Add Round' to create a new round
breakPredict.instruction3: Enter position (1st-4th) and points (0-3) for each team
breakPredict.instruction4: Check predictions in the Predictions tab
breakPredict.addRoundsHint: Add teams first, then create rounds
breakPredict.addDataForPredictions: Add teams and round results to see predictions
breakPredict.confirmRemoveTeam: Are you sure you want to remove this team?
breakPredict.confirmClearAllData: Are you sure you want to clear all data? This action cannot be undone.
